title: Model workstation
description: Configure type = MODEL_WORKSTATION for model-oriented prompts, output parsing, and stop words through a MODEL_WORKER backend.
openingSummary: MODEL_WORKSTATION is the WorkstationType for model-oriented prompt files, outcome parsing, output schemas, and stop words. Set type to MODEL_WORKSTATION when a step must run through a MODEL_WORKER with those prompt/parse surfaces, and choose behavior independently for STANDARD, REPEATER, CRON, or POLLER scheduling. Keep provider-neutral operation bindings on MODEL_INVOKE instead.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Author a workstation with type MODEL_WORKSTATION and bind a MODEL_WORKER. Choose behavior for the scheduling axis you need — STANDARD for fire-once dispatch, REPEATER for change-triggered loops, CRON for schedules, or POLLER for long-lived poller ingress. Wire inputs plus outputs, onContinue, onRejection, and onFailure so model outcomes route correctly. Set promptFile for the authored prompt, and use outcomeFormat, outputSchema, or stopWords when this step needs structured parse or stop control. Do not set operation, operationBindings, openCodeAgent, classificationRoutes, or cron fields that belong to other WorkstationType or WorkstationKind families. Choose MODEL_INVOKE for provider-neutral operation bindings, AGENT_RUN for agent loops, or INFERENCE_RUN for bounded inference harness dispatch. MODEL_WORKSTATION is not a scheduling behavior and is not MODEL_INVOKE — keep operation and operationBindings on the model-invoke type.

schemaReference.title: Schema reference
schemaReference.body: The embed below shows the live Workstation base definition with MODEL_WORKSTATION overlay applicability. MODEL_WORKSTATION selects the exclusive promptFile, outcomeFormat, outputSchema, and stopWords fields. Excluded fields belong to other WorkstationType families or to CRON scheduling and must not appear on this type. Open the full Factory schema reference for exhaustive field lookup rather than copying contract text into this page.

examples.title: Examples
examples.body: Use the minimal valid example when starting a MODEL_WORKSTATION with behavior STANDARD, a promptFile, and outcome parse fields. The misuse example borrows operation from MODEL_INVOKE — that field is excluded from MODEL_WORKSTATION.

links.companionIntro: MODEL_WORKSTATION requires a MODEL_WORKER companion and is compatible with every published WorkstationKind. Bind the model worker, then choose scheduling on the behavior axis. MODEL_INVOKE also uses MODEL_WORKER but selects operation fields instead of prompt/parse surfaces.
links.misuseNote: operation belongs on MODEL_INVOKE. Keep MODEL_WORKSTATION on identity, worker binding, inputs, outputs, routing fields, and promptFile / outcomeFormat / outputSchema / stopWords only.
